claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by asking: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. His name is Alex and he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Now I need to ask you a few more questions about your situation to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Is Alex living with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me currently.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. This is an important factor in determining eligibility.

**Can you tell me who Alex is currently living with, and what your relationship to Alex is?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with his mum, my ex-partner. I'm his dad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for explaining that. 

**Does Alex's mum currently claim Child Benefit for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not sure if she does or not.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's okay. Let me ask you this:

**Are you contributing to Alex's upkeep? For example, are you providing financial support or paying for things like his food, clothing, or other needs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially to his upkeep.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering these questions. Let me provide you with my assessment.

Based on the information you've provided and the UK Child Benefit rules, I need to explain how eligibility works:

**Child Benefit is generally paid to the person who the child lives with and who is responsible for them.** Since Alex lives with his mum and you are not contributing to his upkeep, you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

The key rules are:
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child
- Priority is given to the person the child lives with
- If a child doesn't live with you, you can only claim if you're contributing at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay towards the child's upkeep

Since Alex lives with his mum and you're not providing financial support, his mum would be the person who could claim Child Benefit for him (if she isn't already doing so).

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if Alex comes to live with you, or if you start contributing financially to his upkeep at a level equivalent to Child Benefit - then your eligibility could change and you may wish to reconsider making a claim at that time.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16, meeting the age requirement). However, the claimant (Alex's father) is not eligible for Child Benefit because: 1) Alex does not live with him - Alex lives with his mother, 2) The claimant is not contributing to Alex's upkeep financially. Under UK Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim for a child, and priority goes to the person the child lives with. When a child doesn't live with the claimant, they can only claim if they contribute at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards the child's care. Since the claimant does not meet either criterion (child living with them OR contributing to upkeep), he is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is the father of one child, Alex (age 8), who lives with his mother (the claimant's ex-partner). The claimant does not contribute financially to Alex's upkeep. Under Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim per child, with priority given to the person the child lives with. Since Alex does not live with the claimant and the claimant does not contribute to his upkeep, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

Under UK Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim for a child, and priority goes to the person the child lives with. When a child doesn't live with the claimant, they can only claim if they contribute at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards the child's care. Since the claimant does not meet either criterion (child living with them OR contributing to upkeep), he is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is the father of one child, Alex (age 8), who lives with his mother (the claimant's ex-partner). The claimant does not contribute financially to Alex's upkeep. Under Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim per child, with priority given to the person the child lives with. Since Alex does not live with the claimant and the claimant does not contribute to his upkeep, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
